h1: Calculate Allowed Downtime by SLA Level
description: Calculate allowed downtime minutes by SLA level. Free and instant uptime calculator.
intro: Technical calculations in IT and web infrastructure are essential: latency, bandwidth, uptime SLA, cloud costs, algorithm complexity. Accurate initial planning prevents overprovisioning. Calculate allowed downtime minutes by SLA percentages. Free and instant. Values updated for 2026, reviewed regularly to keep you current.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **Downtime (minutes/year) = (1 − SLA%/100) × 525,600**. Directly apply with your SLA percentage. See how many minutes of downtime are allowed per year.
useCases: Students and IT professionals learning SLA concepts
Teaching and learning SLA uptime basics
explanation:
Technical calculators for IT infrastructure: latency, storage, uptime SLA, cloud costs. Decisions that scale.

## How It's Calculated

Allowed downtime minutes by SLA percentage. This calculator applies the standard formula, checking ranges and alerting if values seem unusual.

## Final Notes

This is a reference tool. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a qualified professional. Values are updated for 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: What does five nines (99.999%) uptime mean?
faq.a: 5.26 minutes of allowed downtime per year. Required for banking and telecom infrastructure.

faq.q: What's the difference between SLA and SLO?
faq.a: SLA is a contractual commitment, SLO is an internal performance objective.
Error budget = 1 − SLO%. It's the time you can fail without breaking your SLA.

faq.q: How can I achieve higher uptime?
faq.a: Use redundancy, multi-region deployment, and automatic failover systems.
AWS offers 99.99% uptime with multiple availability zones in most regions.
